Fa,rn Polrrrc,lr Pnacrrcps ConrurssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
April25,200l
Robin P. Parker, Esq.
New Motor Vehicle Board
$07 2f Street, Suite 330
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-057
Dear Ms. Parker:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I We are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.2
QUESTION
May members of the New Motor Vehicle Board lend the use of a refrigerator to
your agency?
CONCLUSION
If each requirement of Regulationl8944.2 is fulfilled, use of the refrigerator is a
gift to your agency rather than to the individuals using the refrigerator. As such, the gift
rules do not prohibit the members of the New Motor Vehicle Board from lending the use
of a refrigerator in this manner.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Pursuant to enclosed Regulation 18329(c)(3), informal assistance does not confer immunity.


File No. I-01-057
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are staffcounsel with the New Motor Vehicle Board ("Board"). The Board
is a quasi-judicial agency with jurisdiction over the franchise relationship between new
motor vehicle dealers and manufacturers. It also serves as the appellate body for final
decisions issued by the Director of the Department of Motor Vehicles concerning
accusations filed against a dealer or manufacturer license.
The Board consists of five public members and four dealer members. All of the
dealer members and three public members are appointed by the Governor,'bne public
member is appointed by the Speaker of the Assembly, and one is appointed by the Senate
Rules Committee. The Board's Conflict of Interest Code provides, in part, that income
received from a licensed new motor vehicle dealer must be reported. Also, income
received from "an applicant to the Board who has or has had during the filing period a
petition pending before the Board" must be reported.
The Board held eleven meetings in 2000, and has already held three meetings this
year. Meetings are held several times ayear in Sacrameqto. The members voluntarily
contribute to a "coffee kitty." A continental breakfast and refreshments are provided
before a meeting. For meetings held in Sacramento, lunch is ordered in and stored in a
refrigerator in the Board's offices. The current refrigerator is at least 25 least years old.
In an effort to reduce energy consumption in compliance with the Governor's Executive
Orders, the staffunptugged the refrigerator. This poses a problem for members when
meetings are held in Sacramento. Several dealer and public Board members offered to
pay for a refrigerator and lend it to the Board. It would be available for Board member
use when meetings are held in Sacramento, and staffwould also be able to use it. The
arrangements regarding the use of the refrigerator would be such that the members could
request the staffto return the refrigerator at any time.
ANALYSIS
Under the Act, "[n]o member of a state board or commission or designated
employee of a state or local government agency shall accept gifts from any single source
in any calendar year with a total value of more than $3203 if the member or employee
would be required to report the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her
statement of economic interests." (Sections 89503(c).)
Section 82028(a) defines a"gift" as any payment that confers a personal benefit
on the recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received.
Therefore, the use of the refrigerator is considered a gift under the Act. However,
Regulation 18944.2 describes the manner in which a gift is made to a public agency
rather than to a particular public official. We have enclosed this regulation for your
' Th" gft limit amount is adjusted biermially by the Commission to reflect changes in the
Consumer Price lndex. As of January 1, 2001, the amount is $320. (Section 89503(f); Regulation
189a0.2(a).)


File No. I-01-057
Page No. 3
information. If each requirement of this regulation is fulfilled, the gift from the members
is given to your agency and not to the individuals using the property, for purposes of the
Act. This same conclusion would apply if the members were to give, instead of merely
allow the use o[, the refrigerator to your agency.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
0
atalie
StaffCounsel,
Division
